title: Safety, jobs and housing top concerns ahead of Sona
description: GEORGE NEWS - In the lead-up to the State of...
bodyText: [{"type":"contributor","text":"Michelle  Pienaar","contributorName":"Michelle  Pienaar","contributorDate":"Friday, 06 February 2026, 07:00"},{"type":"intro_bold","text":"The Sona will take place at 19:00 on Thursday 12 February. It will be broadcast live on Parliament TV (Channel 408) and live-streamed on Parliament's YouTube channel."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"GEORGE NEWS - In the lead-up to the State of the Nation Address (Sona), Jethro Grootboom, the Government Communication and Information System's (GCIS) regional communications co-ordinator, and Bulumko Kula of Eden FM visited the George Transport Hub to engage with the residents and hear what issues they would like President Cyril Ramaphosa to address.","html":"GEORGE NEWS - In the lead-up to the State of the Nation Address (Sona), Jethro Grootboom, the Government Communication and Information System's (GCIS) regional communications co-ordinator, and Bulumko Kula of Eden FM visited the George Transport Hub to engage with the residents and hear what issues they would like President Cyril Ramaphosa to address."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Safety, job creation and housing opportunities were the top concerns the first 10 individuals interviewed in town brought up."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Sona will take place at 19:00 on Thursday 12 February. It will be broadcast live on Parliament TV (Channel 408) and live-streamed on Parliament's YouTube channel.","html":"The Sona will take place at 19:00 on Thursday 12 February. It will be broadcast live on Parliament TV (Channel 408) and live-streamed on Parliament's YouTube channel."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"George will play the host to the State of the Province Address (Sopa) to be held at the Conville Civic Centre on 25 and","html":"George will play the host to the State of the Province Address (Sopa) to be held at the Conville Civic Centre on 25 and"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"26 February. The proceedings will be live-streamed for public viewing.","html":"26 February. The proceedings will be live-streamed for public viewing."},{"type":"image_with_caption","text":"Thimna Zenzile of George Law Enforcement hands Athenkosi Lamani Rhodes a pamphlet on water management measures.","imageUrl":"https://cms.groupeditors.com/img/32dd22d7-5725-4f96-92c7-2760a1652c92.JPG","caption":"Thimna Zenzile of George Law Enforcement hands Athenkosi Lamani Rhodes a pamphlet on water management measures."},{"type":"image_with_caption","text":"Bulumko Kula (Eden FM), Lamla Viti (Thembalethu), Jethro Grootboom (Goverment Communications), Angeline Lieman (Syferfontein) and Kelly Maraitza (Thembalethu). Photos: Michelle Pienaar","imageUrl":"https://cms.groupeditors.com/img/7ee0d31b-98ee-4acd-92ce-474a5a026607.JPG","caption":"Bulumko Kula (Eden FM), Lamla Viti (Thembalethu), Jethro Grootboom (Goverment Communications), Angeline Lieman (Syferfontein) and Kelly Maraitza (Thembalethu). Photos: Michelle Pienaar"}]

images.alt: Safety, jobs and housing top concerns ahead of Sona
Safety, jobs and housing top concerns ahead of Sona
images.caption: Thimna Zenzile of George Law Enforcement hands Athenkosi Lamani Rhodes a pamphlet on water management measures.

images.alt: Safety, jobs and housing top concerns ahead of Sona
images.caption: Bulumko Kula (Eden FM), Lamla Viti (Thembalethu), Jethro Grootboom (Goverment Communications), Angeline Lieman (Syferfontein) and Kelly Maraitza (Thembalethu). Photos: Michelle Pienaar

author: Michelle  Pienaar
teaser: The Sona will take place at 19:00 on Thursday 12 February. It will be broadcast live on Parliament TV (Channel 408) and live-streamed on Parliament's YouTube channel.
featuredCaption: Jethro Grootboom and Edward Witbooi in conversation at the Transport Hub. Witbooi says he does not support politics because he is already suffering enough. Photo: Michelle Pienaar
